entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company, the domain this position relates to

entities.name: Democratizing recruiter incentives
entities.context: Core thesis: give non-recruiters the same financial incentives and data access that professional recruiters have

entities.name: Data superiority
entities.context: Key advantage traditionally held by recruiters that should be democratized to enable anyone to facilitate hires

entities.name: Incentive alignment
entities.context: Principle that incentive alignment often trumps tool utility in driving behavior

entities.name: Decentralized Finance (DeFi)
entities.context: Analogy used to explain the approach: remove specialized middlemen by giving end-users the same financial instruments

entities.name: The Well-Connected Professional
entities.context: New target persona for the platform - anyone with social networks who could facilitate a hire

entities.name: Internal Recruiter
entities.context: Traditional target persona that this approach deprioritizes

entities.name: Commission tracking
entities.context: Priority feature that enables non-recruiters to claim commissions for facilitating hires

entities.name: Shareability
entities.context: Priority feature direction - making it easy to share opportunities and track referrals

entities.name: Trust graphs
entities.context: Existing social networks of the workforce that can be tapped for recruitment

relationships.context: Core strategic approach for disrupting recruitment
Based on principle that incentive alignment trumps tool utility
Follows same logic of removing specialized middlemen by democratizing their instruments
New target persona replacing Internal Recruiter
Both are priority features over recruiter-specific CRM features
Leverage existing social networks to facilitate hires
Data access is one of the key advantages to be democratized
